WARNING: These test points are for A2442_CTO (820-02443) ONLY. Page numbers differ from base A2442 (820-02098). SVR AON is 42A design. Component designators are board-specific.
description: Decision trees for 20V DCPS on A2442 CTO. Normal boot current is HIGHER than base A2442 due to 42A SVR AON and extended Viper/Monaco phases. Max variant draws more than Pro.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws nothing. CD3217 or charger circuit completely non-functional.

steps.if_wrong: Charger IC not converting. Check CHGR_AUX_OK, I2C to charger.
U5700 (42A CTO) not starting. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN.
steps.if_present: PMU not loading -> check U7700/U8100 connections

scenarios.meaning: Board partially active but well below normal 2-5A boot range. A shorted rail is clamping a regulator.

steps.action: Run full chain: PPBUS -> 3V8 -> 5V -> MPMU -> Viper -> Monaco
steps.next: Identify which stage has low/missing output

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_MPMU, PP3V8_AON_SPMU, PP3V8_AON_WLBT, PP3V8_ATC01_LDO, PP3V8_ATC2_LDO branches
Diode mode on Viper output (CZ610-CZ618) and Monaco output (CA1xx)
steps.if_wrong: VR output shorted. Thermal cam to find hot SoC pin or decoupling cap. CTO has more phases to check (Viper: 3 stage pages, Monaco: 5 instances).

steps.measure: Check PPU815-PPU836 test points (page 135) for each MPMU output
steps.if_any_missing: Thermal cam that rail -> replace hot component or U8100

scenarios.count_note: Below normal boot range for CTO Pro/Max
scenarios.meaning: Board drawing boot-level current for an Air, but insufficient for CTO Pro/Max. SoC partially powered but Viper or Monaco may not be starting.

steps.action: Check Viper VR (U9300) — 3 stage pages CTO
steps.if_missing: Viper not starting. Check U9300, VIPER_ALERT_L. Check power stages (CZ619-CZ654 area, pages 49-51 CTO). This is the most likely cause of stalled boot on CTO Pro/Max at this current range.

steps.measure: Probe Monaco output at CA100 area (page 52)
steps.if_missing: Monaco not starting. MONACO_ALERT_L. CTO Max variant: 5 instances (C0-C4, pages 52-56) -> more failure points than base.

steps.measure: Check NAND power: PP0V9_NAND0, PP0V9_NAND1, PP1V2_NAND0, PP1V2_NAND1 (Ocarina areas pages 106/111)
Backlight on? Image on external HDMI?
steps.if_no_display: Display power sequencer (page 115) or Luxe backlight (page 116) fault

scenarios.meaning: NORMAL for CTO M1 Pro (2-4A) and CTO M1 Max (2.5-5A). CTO Max draws more than base due to extended Monaco/Viper phases. If customer reports a fault, it's peripheral not power.

steps.measure: Display, keyboard, trackpad, speakers, USB-C ports, HDMI, SD card

sub_checks.check: Luxe backlight (page 116), display power sequencer (page 115), eDP connector (page 114)
UF400 (left rear), UF500 (left front), or UG400 (right). Test each.

scenarios.meaning: Excessive even for CTO Max. Major short or GPU under extreme load.

steps.if_functional: May be charging deeply discharged battery. Monitor — should settle to 2-5A.

steps.if_found: Identify component. Common: U5700 42A (regulating into 3V8 short), Viper/Monaco (shorted SoC domain), SoC (dead — BER).

steps.measure: Diode mode: CR765 (PPBUS) -> CZ400 (3V8) -> MC600 (5V) -> CZ610 (Viper) -> CA100 (Monaco)
steps.process: Find shorted rail. Thermal cam that specific area.
